info.title: Catshop REST API
info.description: Entrypoint for Spring-boot. Handles communication between the client and database, as well as hosting the frontend itself.

post.description: Adds specified amount of stock to the database

requestBody.description: JSON object containing product number and quantity of product to buy

Request body.description: Stringified JSON body with product number and quantity wanted

204.description: Successful addition of new stock to database

403.description: Attempt to add less than zero or more than 99 items at a time

post.description: Removes stock from the StockTable, and places it into the OrderTable and BasketTable respectively

requestBody.description: JSON Object containing a basket, an array of objects, each of which have a product number and a quantity to purchase.

Request body.description: Stringified JSON body with product number and quantity wanted

204.description: Empty response, signifies successful purchase on clientside

get.description: Get the current amount of stock in the database, and send it to the client. Used to synchronize customer, cashier and backdoor clients.

200.description: JSON object populated with product names, stock levels and product numbers digested by the client to display stock levels

500.description: This endpoint will return a HTTP 500 error if a connection to the database cannot be made for any reason.

get.summary: Get all items that are waiting to be packed
get.description: Fetch all items in OrderTable, then all items in BasketTable with a matching foreign key to aggregate the baskets correctly

Response body.summary: Array of orders that need to be packed
Response body.description: Array children with identical orderIDs get merged in clientside javascript.

delete.description: Delete orders that are sent to be packed in the packing frontend client.

403.description: Item does not exist in database

Purchase date.description: The date the cashier client processed the purchase, without timestamp.
